integrationKeys.createDescription: Issue a site-level bearer token for the Moodle / Canvas plugin. The plugin uses it to provision per-course API keys on behalf of teachers -- it cannot access course data itself. One key per LMS instance is usually enough.

form.name: Name
form.domainsHelp: Optional. If set, the key can only act on behalf of users whose eppn ends with one of these domains. Leave empty to allow any eppn.

callout.title: Key minted for {{name}}
callout.note: Copy this now; Minerva only stores the hash. Paste it into the Moodle plugin's site settings.

integrationKeys.listDescription: Revoking a key invalidates it immediately. Any api_keys the plugin already provisioned via this site key continue to work (they're ordinary course keys). The eppn scope is immutable; rotate the key to change it.
integrationKeys.empty: No site integration keys minted yet.

ltiPlatforms.setupDescription: Copy these values into Moodle's Site administration → Plugins → Activity modules → External tool → Manage tools, or the equivalent Canvas Developer Key. Any course in the LMS can then use this tool without per-course setup.
ltiPlatforms.siteLevelNote: On first launch from a Moodle course, the launching teacher picks which Minerva course to link to. That choice is saved as a binding and reused for every subsequent launch from the same Moodle course.
ltiPlatforms.listDescription: Each entry corresponds to one Moodle/Canvas instance. Removing a platform cascades to its course bindings; teachers will have to re-link on next launch.
ltiPlatforms.copyValuesHint: Paste the Platform ID (issuer), Client ID and Deployment ID that Moodle/Canvas shows after you save the tool config.
ltiPlatforms.deploymentIdPlaceholder: Leave blank to skip deployment ID check
ltiPlatforms.domainsHelp: Optional. If set, only launches whose claimed eppn ends with one of these domains are accepted. Leave empty to allow any eppn (the legacy behaviour).
ltiPlatforms.noBindings: No Moodle/Canvas courses have been linked to this platform yet.
ltiPlatforms.bindingsHint: Each row is a Moodle/Canvas course that has been linked to a Minerva course on first launch.

users.title: Users ({{total}})
users.description: Manage user roles, daily AI spending limits, and account status. Setting a role manually locks it from auto-promotion rules.

courses.title: Courses ({{total}})
courses.description: All courses on the platform. Click the settings link to open a course's configuration.
courses.migrateButtonTitle: Force-migrate this course onto a different embedding provider/model. Re-queues every document for re-embedding.
courses.migrateDialogTitle: Migrate "{{course}}" to a different embedding model
courses.migrateDialogDescription: Pick the new provider and (for local) model. Admins can target disabled catalog models too -- this is the way to walk a course off a model you've just disabled. Confirming bumps the course's embedding_version, opens a fresh Qdrant collection, and re-queues every document. The previous collection stays on disk (orphaned) so the change is reversible.
courses.migrateWarning: Saving will bump the course's embedding_version and re-queue every document for re-embedding under the new model. Search results will be incomplete until the worker drains the queue (a course with hundreds of documents can take several minutes).
courses.featuresButtonTitle: Toggle which experimental / opt-in features are enabled for this course.
courses.featuresDialogDescription: Per-course feature flags for {{course}}. Changes apply immediately; flipping a flag off reverts the course to the global default for that flag.

featureFlagDescription.course_kg: Per-document kind classification, cross-document linker, knowledge-graph viewer, assignment-refusal addendum, and per-chunk adversarial filter. Off by default while we calibrate.
featureFlagDescription.extraction_guard: Pre-generation intent classifier + output-side solution detector + Socratic rewriter, plus multi-turn assignment-proximity tracking. Catches the 'student pasted assignment, asks for code' case and intercepts the response with a Socratic question. Independent of course_kg so admins can pilot separately.

rules.title: Role Auto-Promotion Rules
rules.description: Each rule promotes a user to its target role when ALL conditions match the user's Shibboleth attributes at login. Rules never demote, never override admins (MINERVA_ADMINS env), and never touch users whose role was set manually in User Management (those users show a "locked" badge; click Unlock there to re-enable rule evaluation). Multi-valued attributes (affiliation, entitlement) use list-membership semantics for "contains"; substring matches are rejected.
rules.negatedNote: Negated operators (<0>not_contains</0>, <1>not_regex</1>) also require the attribute to be present on the user. This prevents external/non-Shib users (who have no affiliation / entitlement / etc. at all) from matching a "not contains X" rule by virtue of having nothing.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Create one above to start auto-promoting users.

card.noConditions: Add at least one condition for this rule to ever match.

externalInvites.createDescription: Generate a time-limited login link for someone without a Stockholm University account. Links bypass Shibboleth and grant student-level access; promote to teacher in User Management after they first log in. The full link is shown only once; if lost, revoke and re-mint.

callout.title: Invite link for {{eppn}}
callout.note: Share this link privately. It is shown only once and grants login until

storage.title: Storage
storage.descriptionTail: ; the host volume holds postgres, qdrant, documents and backups. Contact DSV helpdesk to expand when free space runs low.
storage.nearlyFull: Disk is nearly full. Request expansion from helpdesk soon.
storage.fillingUp: Disk is filling up. Consider requesting expansion.
storage.statsUnavailable: Disk stats unavailable on this platform.

classifications.title: Document classifications
classifications.description: Per-document kinds drive what the chat path will and won't paste into the prompt context. Sample solutions are excluded entirely; assignment briefs trigger a refusal addendum. Newly uploaded docs are classified automatically; this card lets you backfill any docs that landed before the classifier was wired in (or that errored on first try).
classifications.backfillTitle: Run the classifier on every unclassified, unlocked, ready document. Each click queues up to 5,000 docs.
classifications.lastQueued_one: Queued {{count}} doc; status updates as it runs.
classifications.lastQueued_other: Queued {{count}} docs; status updates as it runs.
classifications.note: Each click queues up to 5,000 docs. For larger backlogs, click again once the count has come down.

embeddingModels.title: Embedding models
embeddingModels.description: Catalog of local embedding models the runtime can load. Toggle 'Enabled' to control which ones show up in the per-course teacher dropdown -- existing courses on a model keep working when you disable it, but teachers can't pick it for a new rotation. Throughput numbers come from running each model against a small academic corpus on this server; only the four boot models are benchmarked automatically (loading every catalog entry at boot would OOM-kill the pod), the rest are benchmarked on demand. The server serializes runs, so only one model is loaded at a time.
embeddingModels.runBenchmarkTitle: Load this model and run the benchmark corpus through it. Disabled while another model is benchmarking.
embeddingModels.runningHint: Currently benchmarking {{model}}. The page polls every 1.5s while a run is in flight and will update the row when it finishes.
embeddingModels.note: First run for a model includes a one-time download from HuggingFace, which can take a minute or two for the larger candidates. Subsequent runs are warm.
embeddingModels.confirmDisableTitle: Disable a model that {{count}} course(s) are using?
embeddingModels.confirmDisableBody: The model "{{model}}" is currently selected by {{count}} active course(s). Disabling it removes it from the teacher picker but leaves those courses on it -- they keep working under the model. To move them off, force-migrate each course on the Course Management page (admins can target any catalog model, including disabled ones).
